query: Can you check my Python loop for off-by-one errors? Here it is: for i in range(len(arr)): print(arr[i+1])
expected_behavior: Does NOT spawn the multi-subagent premortem or produce per-round findings tables and a remediation plan for a single off-by-one loop
Addresses the off-by-one directly (arr[i+1] reads one past the end on the final iteration) or hands off to a debugging / code-review approach

Explains that the multi-subagent premortem is for high-stakes designs with multiple plausible failure modes, and may point to adversarial-premortem-single for a structured single-pass review of a larger artifact
